member.name: Bradley Thomas
member.synopsis: Bradley Thomas is the Conservative MP for Bromsgrove , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Gambling: Software
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to stop illegal gambling apps that are using registration; and what support will be put in place for victims of these apps.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The issue of illegal gambling is a concern for this government. We are committed to working closely with the Gambling Commission to ensure that illegal gambling, in all its forms, is addressed. As part of the 2025 Budget, the Treasury announced that the G...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Vetting: Bromsgrove
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the adequacy of the time taken for DBS check processing in Bromsgrove constituency; and what steps she is taking to help reduce that time.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As an Executive Non-Departmental Public Body of the Home Office, the Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) is operationally independent and responsible for the service it provides.DBS does not collate data on processing times by local area or constituency....
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Care Workers: Vetting
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment has been made of the potential impact of processing times for DBS on the trends in the number of carers in the sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As part of the Department’s commissioned research, Understanding recruitment to the adult social care workforce, undertaken by the King’s Fund, Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) checks are referenced in relation to recruitment and retention challenges....
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Members: Correspondence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, when she plans to respond to the correspondence from the hon. Member for Bromsgrove dated 11 November 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department apologies for the significant delay in responding to this correspondence. I provided a full response on 24 June.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Carers: Respite Care
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if his department will consider introducing a short respite service for unpaid carers that they can access yearly.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Responsibility for commissioning respite services rests with local authorities and it is based on their assessment of the needs of their local populations. Through the Better Care Fund, integrated care boards and local authorities are encouraged to work ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: In August 2025 the Government announced its plans to introduce legal restrictions which will ensure that the highest risk cosmetic procedures are brought into Care Quality Commission regulation and can only be performed by specified regulated healthcare p...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Motorway Service Areas: Large Goods Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of trends in the level of crimes targeting lorries at motorway services; what consideration her Department has given to mandating the introduction of security by motorway service pr
writtenQuestions.answerText: Crime recording falls under the remit of the Home Office. Police‑recorded crime data does not separately identify freight crime or its location; therefore, no assessment can be done on the number of incidents at motorway services or analysis of trends ove...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Pensions and Social Security Benefits: Married People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, with reference to the Benefit and Pension Rates 2026-27, updated on 16 February 2026, for what reason his Department has agreed a 4.8% uplift to the maximum weekly allowance for additional spouses; and
writtenQuestions.answerText: State pension and benefit rates are reviewed annually as part of the Secretary of State’s statutory up-rating duty. For 2026/27, Pension Credit-related amounts, including the Standard Minimum Guarantee, were increased by 4.8 per cent in line with the grow...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Heat Pumps
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what estimate he has made of the number of additional heat pumps installed in homes in the financial year 2025/26 as a direct result of the Clean Heat Market Mechanism.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Clean Heat Market Mechanism is designed to support the development of the retrofit heat pump market as a whole, alongside and complementary to other measures such as the Boiler Upgrade Scheme. There was a 24% increase in supported heat pump installati...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is restoring the founding promise of the National Health Service, to bring quality healthcare to all, regardless of how much you earn or where you live. Local integrated care boards (ICBs) are responsible for the design and commissioning of...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: It is the responsibility of integrated care boards (ICBs) to make available appropriate provision to meet the health and care needs of their local population, including provision of attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) services.In respect of AD...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Neurodiversity: Children

writtenQuestions.answerText: We have set clear ambitions for community health services through our Medium Term Planning Framework and for the first time, we have set a target for systems to reduce long waits for community health services. By 2028/29, at least 80% of community health ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Proceedings: Judgements
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of criminal court delays on civil proceedings awaiting those judgements.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government inherited a record and rising Crown Court backlog, with victims facing intolerable delays for justice. We asked Sir Brian Leveson to undertake an Independent Review of the Criminal Courts. In response the Government is delivering record fin...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.answerText: In England, a range of legislation provides strong protections for wildlife species and their habitats. Key frameworks such as the Wildlife and Countryside Act 1981 (WCA) and the Conservation of Habitats and Species Regulations 2017 make it an offence to ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Small Businesses: VAT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of increasing the VAT registration threshold on small businesses; and if she will consider raising the VAT registration threshold to support entrepreneurs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: At £90,000, the UK has a higher VAT registration threshold than any EU country and the joint highest in the OECD. This means the majority of UK businesses are not in the VAT system at all. Any consideration of changes to the threshold would have to carefu...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Prostate Cancer: Screening
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to ensure equitable access to testing for prostate cancer in Bromsgrove constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to improving outcomes for people with prostate cancer and reducing inequalities in access to diagnosis across England, including in Bromsgrove.The National Cancer Plan for England, published in February 2026, sets out action to...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: The police have a duty to pursue all reasonable lines of enquiry, as set out in the Code of Practice under the Criminal Procedure and Investigations Act 1996.The Home Office works with policing and the Crown Prosecution Service to improve all case outcome...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.answerText: Miscarriage can have a devastating impact on women and their families, and we are determined that they receive the support they need. As part of the renewed Women’s Health Strategy, we have committed to closely reviewing the findings presented in the Tomm...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
